title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find out how far the marble travels in $$3.0$$ seconds, we need to consider the motion of the marble as it rolls down the incline. Since it starts from rest, we can use the equations of motion to determine the distance traveled.

title: Equation of Motion
text: The equation of motion for an object starting from rest is: \(d=\frac{1}{2}at**2\), where \(d\) is the distance, \(a\) is the acceleration, and \(t\) is the time.

title: Finding the Acceleration
text: The acceleration \(a\) of the marble can be found using the formula for acceleration down an incline: \(a=g\sin(\theta)\), where $$\(g=9.8\)$$ m/s² and \(\theta=30\) degrees.

title: Calculate the Acceleration
text: Calculate the acceleration of the marble using \(a=g\sin(\theta)\).

title: Calculate the Distance
text: Now that you have the acceleration, use the equation \(d=\frac{1}{2}at**2\) to find the distance traveled in $$3.0$$ seconds.
